item:
Concept: “archived (bookmark boolean property)”
Statement: Whenever someone or something is archived, an archiving happened — something took place in time by which it was put away for keeping. Normally this putting-away is done on purpose, so that the thing is kept but out of the way.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:archived

item:
Concept: “begin (happening X begins at time T)”
Statement: In every case of “begin (happening X begins at time T)” as described above, X is something that happens or goes on in time (a happening or process) — not a person, a place, or a time. Normally X is the kind of happening that lasts for some time once it has begun.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:begin

item:
Concept: “begin (happening X begins at time T)”
Statement: In every case of “begin (happening X begins at time T)” as described above, T is a time — a moment or stretch of time. Normally T is thought of as the moment the happening starts.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:begin

item:
Concept: “believe (X believes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “believe (X believes Y)” as described above, X is someone or something that exists on its own and lasts through time — not a time, a place, or a mere happening. Normally X is someone who can think — a person, or a being or group with a mind.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:believe

item:
Concept: “believe (X believes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “believe (X believes Y)” as described above, Y is something that can be thought — a content, the kind of thing that could also be put into words. Normally Y is something that can be so or not so — the kind of content that can be true or false.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:believe

item:
Concept: “birth (the event)”
Statement: Every birth is a happening — something that takes place in time (an event), not a lasting object. Normally a birth is a bodily happening in the life of a living thing.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:birth

item:
Concept: “birth (the event)”
Statement: In every case of a birth, it happens at some time — there is a time at which the birth takes place. Normally one can say when it happened, at least roughly.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:birth

item:
Concept: “bookmark”
Statement: Every bookmark is a kept mark or record — something that stands for something else so that someone can come back to it; it is not itself a happening or a time. Normally a bookmark is a small piece of stored information — a mark, a note, or an entry that carries what it says in signs or words.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:bookmark

item:
Concept: “bookmark”
Statement: In every case of a bookmark, it was made — a making or saving of it happened at some time. Normally it is made on purpose, so that something can be found again.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:bookmark

item:
Concept: “bookmark”
Statement: In every case of a bookmark, there is something the bookmark marks or points to — something other than the bookmark itself. Normally what it points to is a place in something made of words or signs — a page, a spot, an address.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:bookmark

item:
Concept: “break (X breaks Y)”
Statement: In every case of “break (X breaks Y)” as described above, X is what makes the breaking happen — someone or something. Normally X is someone — a person or other living being — acting on the thing.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:break

item:
Concept: “break (X breaks Y)”
Statement: In every case of “break (X breaks Y)” as described above, Y is a thing that can be in one piece and then not — something with a body or material to it, not a time and not a bare idea. Normally Y is a whole object — one thing that comes apart or stops holding together.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:break

item:
Concept: “break (X breaks Y)”
Statement: In every case of “break (X breaks Y)” as described above, the breaking happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place. Normally one can say when it happened, at least roughly.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:break

item:
Concept: “broken”
Statement: Whenever someone or something is broken, being broken is a way that thing now is — a condition of it, not something separate from it. Normally it is broken because at some earlier time a breaking happened to it.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:broken

item:
Concept: “cause (happening X causes happening Y)”
Statement: In every case of “cause (happening X causes happening Y)” as described above, X is something that happens or is the case — a happening or situation, not a lasting object, a place, or a time. Normally X is itself a happening — something going on that makes the other happening come about.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:cause

item:
Concept: “cause (happening X causes happening Y)”
Statement: In every case of “cause (happening X causes happening Y)” as described above, Y is something that happens or comes about — a happening or outcome, not a lasting object, a place, or a time. Normally Y is a happening that would not have come about, or not in that way, without X.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:cause

item:
Concept: “cause (happening X causes happening Y)”
Statement: In every case of “cause (happening X causes happening Y)” as described above, the causing happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place. Normally one can say when it happened, at least roughly.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:cause

item:
Concept: “celebration”
Statement: Every celebration is a happening — something people do that takes place in time (an event). Normally it is a social happening: people together doing something because something good happened or matters to them.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:celebration

item:
Concept: “celebration”
Statement: In every case of a celebration, something happens — there are things done as part of it. Normally these are things people do on purpose — the celebrating itself.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:celebration

item:
Concept: “change (the event)”
Statement: In every case of a change, there is something that changes — something that was one way before, and another way after. Normally that something is there both before and after — it lasts through the change.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:change

item:
Concept: “change (the event)”
Statement: In every case of a change, it happens at some time — there is a time at which the change takes place. Normally one can say when it happened, at least roughly.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:change

item:
Concept: “condolence (the words)”
Statement: Every condolence is something said — words, spoken or written, offered to someone. Normally the words say that the one offering them feels for someone over something bad — most often a death or a loss.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:condolence

item:
Concept: “condolence (the words)”
Statement: In every case of a condolence, the words are offered to someone — there is one the condolence is for. Normally that one is a person other than the one speaking — the bereaved or the one suffering the loss.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:condolence

item:
Concept: “condolence (the words)”
Statement: In every case of a condolence, someone offers the words — there is a speaker or writer of them. Normally a person, or people, expressing their sympathy.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:condolence

item:
Concept: “conversation”
Statement: Every conversation is a happening — an exchange that takes place in time (an event). Normally it is a social happening: people speaking with one another.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:conversation

item:
Concept: “conversation”
Statement: In every case of a conversation, words are said to someone — those speaking are speaking to someone. Normally to someone else: a conversation has more than one person in it.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:conversation

item:
Concept: “conversation”
Statement: In every case of a conversation, someone says things — there is at least one person speaking. Normally the ones in it are persons speaking with words.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:conversation

item:
Concept: “death (the event)”
Statement: Every death is a happening — something that takes place in time (an event). Normally it is the ending of a life — a bodily happening in a living thing.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:death

item:
Concept: “death (the event)”
Statement: In every case of a death, something living dies — there is one whose death it is. Normally a living thing — a person, an animal, a plant.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:death

item:
Concept: “death (the event)”
Statement: In every case of a death, it happens at some time — there is a time at which the death takes place. Normally one can say when it happened, at least roughly.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:death

item:
Concept: “end (happening X ends at time T)”
Statement: In every case of “end (happening X ends at time T)” as described above, X is something that happens or goes on in time (a happening or process) — not a person, a place, or a time. Normally X is the kind of happening that was going on for some time before it ended.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:end

item:
Concept: “end (happening X ends at time T)”
Statement: In every case of “end (happening X ends at time T)” as described above, T is a time — a moment or stretch of time. Normally T is thought of as the moment the happening stops.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:end

item:
Concept: “event”
Statement: In every case of a event, it happens at some time — there is a time at which the event takes place. Normally one can say when it happened, at least roughly.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:event

item:
Concept: “find (X finds Y)”
Statement: In every case of “find (X finds Y)” as described above, X is the one that does the finding — someone or something that can look for things or come upon them; not a time or a place. Normally X is a person, or an animal or other being that can perceive.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:find

item:
Concept: “find (X finds Y)”
Statement: In every case of “find (X finds Y)” as described above, Y is what is found — something that was not in view, or not known to be where it is, and then is. Normally Y is a thing, but it can be a person, an animal, a place, or even something like an answer.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:find

item:
Concept: “forget (X forgets Y)”
Statement: In every case of “forget (X forgets Y)” as described above, X is the one who forgets — someone, or some being, that could know or remember things; not a time, a place, or a mere thing. Normally X is a person or other thinking being.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:forget

item:
Concept: “forget (X forgets Y)”
Statement: In every case of “forget (X forgets Y)” as described above, Y is what is forgotten — something that was known, remembered, or meant to be done. Normally Y is something that can be held in mind: a fact, a name, a plan, a happening.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:forget

item:
Concept: “friend (X is a friend of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “friend (X is a friend of Y)” as described above, X is a being one can have a bond with — someone, not a mere thing, a time, or a place. Normally X is a person; people also speak of animals, or of groups, as friends.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:friend

item:
Concept: “friend (X is a friend of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “friend (X is a friend of Y)” as described above, Y is a being one can have a bond with — someone, not a mere thing, a time, or a place. Normally Y is a person; people also speak of animals, or of groups, as friends.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:friend

item:
Concept: “gift”
Statement: In every case of a gift, there is someone the gift is for — one meant to have it. Normally someone other than the giver, and the giver wants them to have it.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:gift

item:
Concept: “gift”
Statement: In every case of a gift, a giving is in play — the gift is given, or meant to be given. Normally the giving is done freely, with nothing owed for it.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:gift

item:
Concept: “give (X gives Y to someone)”
Statement: In every case of “give (X gives Y to someone)” as described above, X is the giver — the one from whom Y goes; someone or something that can have and hand over. Normally X is a person or a group of people.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:give

item:
Concept: “give (X gives Y to someone)”
Statement: In every case of “give (X gives Y to someone)” as described above, Y is what is given — something that can go from one to another. Normally Y is a thing that can be had or kept.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:give

item:
Concept: “give (X gives Y to someone)”
Statement: In every case of “give (X gives Y to someone)” as described above, the giving happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place. Normally one can say when it happened, at least roughly.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:give

item:
Concept: “grieving”
Statement: Whenever someone or something is grieving, they feel something very bad over one that is gone — grieving is a state they are in. Normally the grieving follows a death — of a person or other loved being — or another deep loss, and the loss came before the grieving.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:grieving

item:
Concept: “has part (X has part Y)”
Statement: In every case of “has part (X has part Y)” as described above, X is a whole — something with parts; it can be a thing, a person, a place, or even a happening. Normally X is one thing whose parts belong to it and make it up.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:has-part

item:
Concept: “has part (X has part Y)”
Statement: In every case of “has part (X has part Y)” as described above, Y is a part — something that belongs to X and helps make it up; it can be a thing, a person, a place, or even a happening. Normally Y is a smaller piece or portion of the whole.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:has-part

item:
Concept: “help (X helps Y)”
Statement: In every case of “help (X helps Y)” as described above, X is what does the helping — someone or something that makes things better or easier for Y. Normally X is a person or other being acting on purpose.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:help

item:
Concept: “help (X helps Y)”
Statement: In every case of “help (X helps Y)” as described above, Y is the one helped — someone or something whose doing or situation the help is for. Normally Y is a person or a group of people.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:help

item:
Concept: “inside (X is inside Y)”
Statement: In every case of “inside (X is inside Y)” as described above, X is something with a place — a thing, a person, or a stuff that is somewhere; not a time. Normally X is a thing or being that could also be elsewhere.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:inside

item:
Concept: “inside (X is inside Y)”
Statement: In every case of “inside (X is inside Y)” as described above, Y is something with an inside — a thing, a place, or a container that can hold or surround; not a time. Normally Y has bounds — walls, edges, or a surface — with room within them.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:inside

item:
Concept: “kind (gufo:Kind, sortal type)”
Statement: In every case of a kind, there are — or could be — things of that kind: a kind is a way things are grouped as being alike.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:kind

item:
Concept: “learn (X learns Y)”
Statement: In every case of “learn (X learns Y)” as described above, X is the learner — someone or something that can come to know; not a time, a place, or a mere thing. Normally X is a person or other being with a mind — an animal can learn too.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:learn

item:
Concept: “learn (X learns Y)”
Statement: In every case of “learn (X learns Y)” as described above, Y is what is learned — something that can be known or done: a fact, a skill, a way. Normally Y is content one can carry in mind — knowledge or know-how.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:learn

item:
Concept: “lie (the words)”
Statement: Every lie is something said — made of words, spoken or written. Normally the words say something the one saying them takes to be not true.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:lie

item:
Concept: “lie (the words)”
Statement: In every case of a lie, the words are said to someone — a lie is told. Normally to someone else — though one can, in a stretched way, lie to oneself.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:lie

item:
Concept: “lie (the words)”
Statement: In every case of a lie, someone tells it — there is the one whose words they are. Normally a person speaking or writing.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:lie

item:
Concept: “lose (X loses Y)”
Statement: In every case of “lose (X loses Y)” as described above, X is the one who loses — someone or something that had Y and then does not. Normally X is a person, or a group, to whom Y belonged or mattered.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:lose

item:
Concept: “lose (X loses Y)”
Statement: In every case of “lose (X loses Y)” as described above, Y is what is lost — something X had, held, or kept. Normally Y is a thing that can be had — but it can also be a person kept track of, or something like time, a game, or a way.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:lose

item:
Concept: “lost”
Statement: Whenever someone or something is lost, being lost is how things now stand with it — it cannot be found by, or does not know its way to, those it matters to. Normally this came about through an earlier losing — someone lost it, or it lost its way.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:lost

item:
Concept: “make (X makes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “make (X makes Y)” as described above, X is the maker — what brings Y into being; someone or something. Normally X is a person, or people, working on purpose.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:make

item:
Concept: “make (X makes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “make (X makes Y)” as described above, Y is what is made — something that comes to be through the making. Normally Y is a thing one can point to afterwards — an object, a stuff, a work.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:make

item:
Concept: “make (X makes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “make (X makes Y)” as described above, the making happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place. Normally one can say when it happened, at least roughly.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:make

item:
Concept: “maker of (X is the maker of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “maker of (X is the maker of Y)” as described above, X is the maker of Y — the one that brought Y into being. Normally X is a person or people.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:maker-of

item:
Concept: “maker of (X is the maker of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “maker of (X is the maker of Y)” as described above, Y is what was made — something that came to be through X’s making. Normally Y is a thing one can point to — an object, a stuff, a work.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:maker-of

item:
Concept: “maker of (X is the maker of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “maker of (X is the maker of Y)” as described above, a making happened — Y came to be through it, at some time. Normally the making was done by X on purpose.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:maker-of

item:
Concept: “memory (a stored something)”
Statement: In every case of a memory, the memory is of something — there is something it keeps or is about. Normally something from before — a happening, a person, a place, a fact — as it was taken in.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:memory

item:
Concept: “near (X is near Y)”
Statement: In every case of “near (X is near Y)” as described above, X is something with a place — a thing, a person, or a place itself; not a time. Normally X is a thing or being located somewhere.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:near

item:
Concept: “near (X is near Y)”
Statement: In every case of “near (X is near Y)” as described above, Y is something with a place — a thing, a person, or a place itself; not a time. Normally Y is a thing, a being, or a spot that other things can be close to.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:near

item:
Concept: “part of (X is part of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “part of (X is part of Y)” as described above, X is a part — something that belongs to Y and helps make it up; it can be a thing, a person, a place, or even a happening. Normally X is a smaller piece or portion of the whole.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:part-of

item:
Concept: “part of (X is part of Y)”
Statement: In every case of “part of (X is part of Y)” as described above, Y is a whole — something the part belongs to; it can be a thing, a person, a place, or even a happening. Normally Y is one thing whose parts belong to it and make it up.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:part-of

item:
Concept: “promise (the words)”
Statement: Every promise is something said — made of words, spoken, written, or clearly signalled. Normally the words bind the one saying them to do, or not do, something.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:promise

item:
Concept: “promise (the words)”
Statement: In every case of a promise, it is made to someone — there is one to whom the word is given. Normally someone else; one can also promise oneself.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:promise

item:
Concept: “promise (the words)”
Statement: In every case of a promise, someone makes it — there is the one who gives their word. Normally a person, or a group speaking as one.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:promise

item:
Concept: “remember (X remembers Y)”
Statement: In every case of “remember (X remembers Y)” as described above, X is the one who remembers — someone, or some being, in whose mind Y is kept or comes back; not a time, a place, or a mere thing. Normally X is a person or other being with a mind.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:remember

item:
Concept: “remember (X remembers Y)”
Statement: In every case of “remember (X remembers Y)” as described above, Y is what is remembered — something that can be held in mind: a fact, a happening, a person, a place, a thing to do. Normally Y is content from before — something X knew, saw, or went through.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:remember

item:
Concept: “reminder (the made something)”
Statement: In every case of a reminder, it was made or set up — something was done so that someone will call something back to mind. Normally by someone — a person — often through a tool or device that then does the reminding.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:reminder

item:
Concept: “reminder (the made something)”
Statement: In every case of a reminder, there is something it is meant to bring back to mind — the reminder is about, or for, something else. Normally something to be done, or something not to be forgotten.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:reminder

item:
Concept: “repair (X repairs Y)”
Statement: In every case of “repair (X repairs Y)” as described above, X is the one that does the repairing — someone or something that works on Y so that it is right again. Normally X is a person, or people, with the needed skill.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:repair

item:
Concept: “repair (X repairs Y)”
Statement: In every case of “repair (X repairs Y)” as described above, Y is what is repaired — a thing that was working or whole, then was not, and is worked on to be so again. Normally Y is a made thing — something built or put together that can go wrong.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:repair

item:
Concept: “repair (X repairs Y)”
Statement: In every case of “repair (X repairs Y)” as described above, the repairing happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place. Normally one can say when it happened, at least roughly.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:repair

item:
Concept: “take (X takes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “take (X takes Y)” as described above, X is the taker — the one that comes to have or hold Y; someone or something that can do so. Normally X is a person or other living being.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:take

item:
Concept: “take (X takes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “take (X takes Y)” as described above, Y is what is taken — something that can be had, held, or carried off. Normally Y is a thing; it can also be a person led along, or something like time or a chance.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:take

item:
Concept: “take (X takes Y)”
Statement: In every case of “take (X takes Y)” as described above, the taking happens at some time — there is a time at which it takes place. Normally one can say when it happened, at least roughly.
subject: urn:kernel-v0:take
